title: Valley - The Kathmandu Post
summary: Valley Kathmandu police map 1,097 repeat offenders By Gaurav Pokharel Profile list covers people linked to gangs, theft and fraud, aiming to guide future investigations. Local units are defying the federal school calendar. Bhaktapur follows suit By Sundar Shilpakar Bhaktapur joins several municipalities and rural municipalities in setting their own academic schedules, amid disputes over enrolment
content:
Valley Kathmandu police map 1,097 repeat offenders By Gaurav Pokharel Profile list covers people linked to gangs, theft and fraud, aiming to guide future investigations. Local units are defying the federal school calendar. Bhaktapur follows suit By Sundar Shilpakar Bhaktapur joins several municipalities and rural municipalities in setting their own academic schedules, amid disputes over enrolment dates, start of classes and weekly holidays. How a wrong number led to a deadly street clash in Lalitpur By Gaurav Pokharel A misdialled call escalated into insults, a face-to-face meeting and a sudden street fight in Mangalbazar that ended with two brothers being killed. French national found in Pashupatinath area handed over to immigration, says Nepal Police By Post Report Police say the individual had been living in Nepal for six years and left belongings at a hotel due to financial difficulties, urging the public not to spread unverified content. Maternity hospital to manage medical waste of KMC’s urban health promotion centres By Post Report Along with private health facilities, some state-run health facilities also mix up hazardous medical waste with household waste. RSP’s Jagdish Kharel wins Lalitpur-2 by wide margin By Post Report Former communications minister secures 39,491 votes, defeating Nepali Congress rival Prem Krishna Maharjan. RSP’s Rukesh Ranjit defeats Prem Suwal in Bhaktapur-1 By Post Report Nepal Majdoor Kishan Party veteran Suwal loses as Ranjit secures 33,436 votes. RSP’s Dol Prasad Aryal wins Kathmandu-9 By Post Report Aryal secures victory by a margin of nearly 30,000 votes over Congress rival. RSP’s Pukar Bam wins Kathmandu-4 By Post Report Nepali Congress and UML candidates trail far behind in final tally. RSP’s Raju Nath Pandey defeats Kulman Ghising in Kathmandu-3 By Post Report Rastriya Swatantra Party candidate secures victory with 18,757 votes; Ujyalo Nepal Party chair finishes second. RSP’s Ranju Darshana wins Kathmandu-1 By Post Report Nepali Congress candidate Prabal Thapa finishes second in final tally. Ranju Darshana close to victory in Kathmandu-1 as counting nears completion By Post Report RSP candidate holds commanding lead with around 8,000 votes left to be counted. RSP widens lead in Kathmandu-10 By Post Report Pradeep Bista ahead by 5,000 votes. RSP widens lead in Kathmandu-7 By Post Report Ganesh Parajuli strengthens early advantage with over 4,600 votes Rastriya Swatantra Party leads in both Bhaktapur constituencies By Post Report Rukesh Ranjit and Rajiv Khatri ahead in Bhaktapur-1 and Bhaktapur-2, respectively. Biraj Bhakta Shrestha surges ahead in Kathmandu-8 By Post Report Rastriya Swatantra Party candidate maintains strong lead over Congress and UML rivals. Raju Pandey takes lead over Kulman Ghising in Kathmandu-3 By Post Report Pandey surpasses Ghising, who held early lead, in preliminary vote count. Nepali Congress’ Udaya Shumsher Rana leads in Lalitpur-1 By Post Report Rana ahead of UML’s Chetanath Sanjel in early vote count. Rastriya Swatantra Party’s Pukar Bam takes early lead in Kathmandu-4 By Post Report Bam ahead of UML’s Rajan Bhattarai as initial vote count is released. Rastriya Swatantra Party’s Pradeep Bista leads in Kathmandu-10 By Post Report Bista takes early lead as vote counting begins in the constituency.
